title: Seattle → Vancouver & Whistler · 4 Days (EV, cross-border)
subtitle: Seattle → Vancouver, BC → Whistler → back · electric, two border crossings

routeOptions.name: Sea-to-Sky loop (I-5 + Hwy 99)
routeOptions.pros: open all year, fully EV-charged corridor

routeOptions.name: + North Cascades Hwy (SR-20) return
routeOptions.cons: SR-20 CLOSED in winter; sparse EV charging

docs: Valid passport (or NEXUS / enhanced driver's license at land crossings)
Birth certificate + photo ID for minors; consent letter if a child travels without both parents
vehicleDocs: Rental agreement that EXPLICITLY permits crossing into Canada (many US rentals require a cross-border authorization letter)
crossings.insuranceNote: US auto insurance is generally valid in Canada — carry a Canadian Non-Resident Inter-Province Motor Vehicle Liability Insurance Card (ask your insurer) as proof. Confirm coverage before you go.
customsNotes: Declare all food, alcohol, tobacco, and cash > CAD $10,000
Cannabis is illegal to carry across the border in EITHER direction
Firearms must be declared and most handguns are prohibited
Radar detectors are illegal in several provinces
crossings.estWaitNote: Border waits swing from minutes to 2+ hours by port/time of day — check live before you go and avoid weekend/holiday peaks.
crossings.disclaimer: Entry rules change frequently — confirm with CBP / CBSA / Mexican customs (Banjercito) before travel.

docs: Valid passport / passport card / NEXUS / enhanced ID (WHTI-compliant)
Visa or ESTA if applicable to your nationality
crossings.insuranceNote: Carry proof of US-valid auto insurance. If entering from Mexico, your Mexican policy will not cover you in the US.
customsNotes: Declare all agricultural products, food, and cash > USD $10,000
Cannabis remains federally illegal to bring into the US
Have your CBP declaration ready; consider Global Entry to speed re-entry
crossings.waitTimes: CBP Border Wait Times (bwt.cbp.gov) / CBP One app
crossings.estWaitNote: Border waits swing from minutes to 2+ hours by port/time of day — check live before you go and avoid weekend/holiday peaks.
crossings.disclaimer: Entry rules change frequently — confirm with CBP / CBSA / Mexican customs (Banjercito) before travel.

legs.chargeNote: charge to ~49% (100kW) before the next leg
charge to ~34% (50kW) before the next leg
charge to ~54% (100kW) before the next leg
charge to ~52% (150kW) before the next leg

evPlan.note: Linear range model for planning only — verify with ABRP / the car's planner.

days.title: Seattle → Vancouver, BC (cross border)
days.timezoneNote: Same timezone (PT); units switch to km/°C/CAD in Canada

stops.name: Seattle (start)
stops.note: Charge to ~90% before leaving; fuel/charge cheaper on the US side.

stops.name: Bellingham, WA (charge + lunch)
stops.note: Last US charge stop; Fairhaven district for lunch.

stops.name: Peace Arch / Blaine border
stops.note: Busiest BC crossing — check live wait; Nexus lane if eligible.

stops.name: Vancouver, BC
stops.note: Check in; walk the seawall / Gastown.

fuelCharging.name: Charge @ Bellingham (Electrify America)
fuelCharging.note: top up before the border; chargers are pricier/scarcer in the city core.

risks: Peace Arch backups can exceed an hour on weekends/holidays — cross mid-morning weekday if you can.
Carry the rental's cross-border authorization letter; without it you can be turned back.

days.title: Vancouver city day

stops.name: Stanley Park
stops.note: Seawall loop by bike or car; totem poles.

stops.name: Capilano Suspension Bridge
stops.note: Buy timed tickets online to skip the line.

risks: Downtown Vancouver parking is expensive — transit/walk the core; some hotels charge nightly for charging.

days.title: Vancouver → Whistler (Sea-to-Sky Hwy 99)

stops.name: Shannon Falls / Sea to Sky Gondola, Squamish
stops.note: Stretch + huge falls; gondola for the view.

stops.name: Whistler Village
stops.note: Walk the village; off-season rates are far lower than winter.

risks: Sea-to-Sky is stunning but winding with rockslide zones — drive it in daylight.
Fewer fast chargers north of Squamish — arrive in Whistler with margin.

days.title: Whistler → Seattle (return, cross border)
days.timezoneNote: Units switch back to mi/°F/USD after re-entering the US

stops.name: Squamish (charge)
stops.note: Charge before the long run south.

stops.name: Peace Arch / Blaine border (southbound)
stops.note: Declare goods; US re-entry, have CBP declaration ready.

risks: Longest drive of the trip + a border crossing — leave Whistler by late morning to beat afternoon border and I-5 traffic.

bookingCountdown.item: Mexican/Canadian insurance — N/A; confirm US policy covers Canada
bookingCountdown.note: Get the Canadian Non-Resident insurance card before departure.
Must explicitly permit driving into Canada.
Whistler is cheap off-season but books up on weekends.

items.label: EV charging (~450 mi public DC, est.)

tips: US auto insurance is usually valid in Canada — but carry the Canadian Non-Resident Inter-Province insurance card as proof. (Mexico is the opposite: US insurance is NOT valid there.)
Cannabis cannot cross the border in either direction, even between two places where it's legal.
In Canada everything is km/°C/CAD and fuel/charging is sold per litre/kWh — the page flags the switch on the relevant days.
EV range drops ~20–25% in winter; this May plan keeps a 10% buffer per leg, but re-run the corridor with a winter derate if you go in the cold.
Sea-to-Sky (Hwy 99) is plowed year-round, but the scenic North Cascades Hwy (SR-20) alternative is CLOSED in winter — see the route comparison.
disclaimer: AI-assembled from general knowledge; border rules, EV charger availability, prices, and road conditions change fast. Confirm with CBP/CBSA, your insurer, PlugShare/ABRP, and your nav app before and during the trip.
